# UX Requirements Checklist: Claude Code Marketplace Installation Path

**Purpose**: Validate that DOC-003 requirements describe a first-time Claude Code install path that is ordered, concrete, low-risk, and clear about trust without obscuring the canonical install flow.
**Created**: 2026-06-14
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are the required first-time user steps ordered as add marketplace, install SpecKit Pro, reload plugins, and verify the result? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002]
- [x] CHK002 Are update, uninstall, marketplace removal, and reinstall requirements included without requiring users to infer lifecycle order? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK003 Are the canonical Claude route and Codex cross-link boundaries specified without duplicating Codex procedure detail? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-012]
- [x] CHK004 Does the spec define where deep trust content belongs so the first-time install flow is not interrupted while evaluators can still review trust surfaces before running plugin skills? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008, Plan §Structure Decision]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK005 Are exact user-facing commands required for each install, verification, update, uninstall, removal, and reinstall action? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003, Data Model §Lifecycle Command Set]
- [x] CHK006 Are expected successful verification signals required in plain language for `/plugin`, `/speckit-pro:speckit-status`, and `/speckit-pro:speckit-coach`? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-005]
- [x] CHK007 Is the uninstall-vs-marketplace-removal distinction clear enough for users who may keep other Racecraft plugins later? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK008 Do the spec, plan, quickstart, and design concept consistently treat Claude Code as the only detailed install path in DOC-003? [Consistency, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Constraints, Quickstart §Scenario 3]
- [x] CHK009 Do the requirements consistently use current namespaced skill terminology instead of deprecated command-folder wording for user-facing Claude verification? [Consistency, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-011]
- [x] CHK010 Do the trust requirements consistently distinguish official Claude platform authority from Racecraft repository source and generated payload authority? [Consistency, Spec §FR-010, Research §Decision: Use official Claude Code docs plus repository source/generated payload citations]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK011 Are the time-based success criteria for first-time installation and trust-surface identification objectively measurable from the canonical page? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001, Spec §SC-003]
- [x] CHK012 Are docs-only scope criteria measurable enough to show that runtime behavior, generated payloads, versions, and release automation remain untouched? [Measurability, Spec §SC-006, Plan §Declared File Operations]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK013 Are already-added marketplace, stale plugin list, pre-install evaluator review, uninstall-with-marketplace-retained, platform-doc drift, and generated-path distinction edge cases covered? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK014 Are low-risk verification scenarios covered through plugin UI inspection plus the status and coach skill checks? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 2, Quickstart §Scenario 2]

## Notes

- Checklist focus areas: first-time flow order, canonical path clarity, low-risk verification, lifecycle sequence, and deep trust placement.
- Depth level: Standard reviewer gate for a docs/process feature.
- Actor/timing: PR reviewer validating requirements before task generation or implementation.
- Re-run result: CHK004 passed after `spec.md` FR-008 and `plan.md` Structure Decision required progressive disclosure for trust content.
